title: Atlas of the Heart: Mapping Meaningful Connection and the Language of Human Experience
subtitle: A comprehensive map of 87 emotions and experiences — the language we need to understand ourselves and connect with others.
author: Brené Brown
themes: Language shapes emotional experience — naming emotions gives us power over them
The 87 emotions and experiences that define human connection
The difference between empathy and sympathy, shame and guilt, envy and jealousy
Vulnerability as the birthplace of connection
The courage to tell the story of who you are with your whole heart
excerpt: Brené Brown spent two decades researching the emotions and experiences that define what it means to be human. In Atlas of the Heart, she maps 87 distinct emotions, organizing them into categories that reveal how they connect and differ. The result is an atlas of human experience — a vocabulary for understanding ourselves and a compass for navigating connection with others.
